title: Youth Enrichment Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Youth Enrichment Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ADELANTE MUJERES CHICAS YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth development program helping Latina girls develop their leadership potential, adopt healthy lifestyles, develop cultural identity, and achieve academic success with high school graduation and college enrollment. Provides an environment in which young Latinas learn from each other and, through their facilitators, what it means to be a successful person, explore career options, and prepare for higher education. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Latina students in grades 3-12 at participating schools Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm. Program hours include evenings and weekends. Email (503) 992-0078 204 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 3460 Midvale Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS LEBANON OFFICE COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 500 Weldwood Drive Building E Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY YOUTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supplemental education and reinforcement of academics while providing opportunities for youth to explore new subjects through STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Mathematics) workshops and Lego Robotic groups. 1110 N Adair Street Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: Grades 3-8 Hours: Registration: Monday-Friday 9am to 5:30pm Program: Saturdays 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 359-0446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK CONEXIONES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A culturally specific after-school program focused on service learning and academic success. Students meet twice a week to experience teamwork by collaborating in interactive pro-social activities and cultural awareness and self-identity activities. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Latino students in grades 7-8 Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK NINTH GRADE COUNTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Summer enrichment program for incoming freshmen transitioning into high school. Students learn about time management, internships, writing scholarship essays, how to succeed in high school, career exploration, healthy sexuality, and Latin American culture. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Incoming Latino freshmen transitioning into 9th grade Hours: Daily for 4 weeks during summer Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADELANTE MUJERES CHICAS YOUTH DEVELOPMENT CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY YOUTH PROGRAM LATINO NETWORK CONEXIONES PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD YOUTH & FAMILIES
